commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a `fix_artifact.repair_contract` schema/prompt/runtime contract, enforces it before each repair checkpoint commit, emits it from two deterministic repair producers, and adds focused repair contract tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch blockers: source inspection shows two producers derive `repair_contract` from `likely_files`, and the new helper enforces that contract at checkpoints. The schema/runtime mismatch is visible from the validator accepting missing `match` and `scope` while the schema requires them.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining blockers are narrow producer and validator repairs that automation can attempt if a maintainer promotes this PR.

target.title: Enforce repair contract at every checkpoint
target.body:
## Summary

Update the repair checkpoint contract work after maintainer review on #340.

This revision no longer relies on unsupported top-level `must_touch` / `must_touch_files` fields. It defines a canonical `fix_artifact.repair_contract` object and wires it through the schema, prompt/output contract, runtime validators, deterministic producers where applicable, executor checkpoint enforcement, and tests.

## What changed since the closed predecessor

```text
- adds canonical fix_artifact.repair_contract to schema/repair/codex-result.schema.json
- keeps schema additionalProperties:false while allowing the new contract object
- documents the contract in the worker final-output prompt
- validates contract shape in execute-fix-validation and review-results
- changes executor checkpoint enforcement to read fix_artifact.repair_contract
- removes unsupported top-level must_touch / must_touch_files reliance
- parses changed files with git status --porcelain=v1 -z --untracked-files=all
- moves contract parsing/enforcement into a focused testable module
- adds functional tests for spaces, rename destinations, any/all matching, and likely_files bypass
- adds repair_contract from deterministic producers only for bounded concrete likely-file sets
```

## Contract shape

```json
"repair_contract": {
  "must_touch": ["path/that/must/change.ts"],
  "match": "any",
  "scope": "every_checkpoint"
}
```

Semantics:

```text
must_touch: non-empty list of safe relative paths
match: any | all
scope: every_checkpoint
```

The executor enforces this only when `fix_artifact.repair_contract` is present. Plain `likely_files` remains advisory and does not activate fail-closed checkpoint behavior.

## Scope

```text
schema/repair/codex-result.schema.json
src/repair/repair-checkpoint-contract.ts
src/repair/execute-fix-artifact.ts
src/repair/execute-fix-validation.ts
src/repair/review-results.ts
src/repair/lib.ts
src/repair/deterministic-automerge-result.ts
src/repair/commit-finding-intake.ts
test/repair/repair-checkpoint-contract.test.ts
test/repair/execute-fix-artifact-source.test.ts
```

## Validation

```text
corepack pnpm run format:check
corepack pnpm run build:repair
node --test \
  test/repair/repair-checkpoint-contract.test.ts \
  test/repair/execute-fix-artifact-source.test.ts \
  test/repair/deterministic-automerge-result.test.ts \
  test/repair/commit-finding-report.test.ts
corepack pnpm run lint:repair
corepack pnpm run test:repair
```

Observed result:

```text
format:check: passed
build:repair: passed
focused tests: 19/19 passed
lint:repair: 0 warnings, 0 errors
test:repair: passed
```

## Local repair-worker dry-run proof

I ran the real `dist/repair/execute-fix-artifact.js` executor locally in `--dry-run` mode with `CODEX_BIN` set to a deterministic fake Codex binary. This exercises the executor, subprocess boundary, git checkout, checkpoint helper and dry-run report path without pushing or opening PRs.

This proof verifies the checkpoint contract boundary. It does not claim provider/model quality or a live Codex-provider run.

### 1. Allowed checkpoint when `repair_contract.must_touch` is changed

Setup:

```text
fix_artifact.repair_contract = {
  must_touch: ["docs/repair-contract-proof.md"],
  match: "any",
  scope: "every_checkpoint"
}
fake worker edit = docs/repair-contract-proof.md
```

Observed result:

```text
exit: 0
report_status: planned
action: open_fix_pr planned
checkpoint commit: 9cdf11e6b647 test: local repair contract e2e proof
git_status after run: clean
```

### 2. Rejected checkpoint when only an off-contract file is changed

Setup:

```text
fix_artifact.repair_contract = {
  must_touch: ["docs/repair-contract-proof.md"],
  match: "any",
  scope: "every_checkpoint"
}
fake worker edit = docs/off-contract-proof.md
```

Observed result:

```text
exit: 1
head remained at base commit: c88270b81889
working tree: ?? docs/off-contract-proof.md
```

Error:

```text
Error: repair checkpoint contract rejected initial: no required repair_contract.must_touch file changed; match=any; must_touch=docs/repair-contract-proof.md; changed_files=docs/off-contract-proof.md
```

## Relation to maintainer review on #340

This revision addresses the two blocking findings from #340:

```text
1. Unsupported fields: replaced top-level must_touch / must_touch_files with canonical fix_artifact.repair_contract in the schema, prompt, validators and producers.
2. Porcelain parser: replaced plain porcelain parsing with git status --porcelain=v1 -z --untracked-files=all and functional parser tests covering spaces and rename destinations.
```

This PR replaces the closed #340/#336 approaches rather than reopening them.
